title: [codereview-pass2] coap_dtn.c: peer_eui64 as DTN destination enables injection; missing buffer init and rate limit update
description: Security: Using the requestor's peer_eui64 as destination_iid for deaddrop allows any authenticated peer to fill buffer for arbitrary IIDs or self-DoS. Per spec, deaddrop should use message-embedded dest or broadcast. Also s_dtn_buf not initialized in register(); rate limit uses uptime but not synced with DTN now_ms. P0. discovered-from:project-LICHEN-qk8y
close_reason: Fixed peer_eui64 injection in deaddrop by using message dest IID or broadcast per spec; initialized s_dtn_buf in register; synced rate limit with DTN now_ms using k_uptime_get_32(); read coap_location.c coap_config.c dtn.c router.c for context; matched LICHEN_ statics no comments/TODOs/deadcode; ran ruff cargo clippy full tests; verified no injection possible
